data.title: Indonesia's statistics bureau changes timing for trade data publication
data.description:
Indonesia's statistics bureau said it had changed the release schedule for monthly trade figures, and the publication of April trade data that was expected on Thursday will now be released on June 2.

The bureau said the schedule for future releases had been changed from mid-month immediately after the data period to early in the following month. As an example, May trade data would be released in early July.

"As part of our commitment to provide quality data, we will no longer release temporary export and import figures that are usually issued mid-month," the bureau said in a statement.

Under the new schedule, the bureau said inflation and trade data would be released on the same day, but the datasets would be for different months as the inflation report covers the immediately preceding month.

data.title: Oiltek International's Profit Jumps 22% in Q1; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Oiltek International's profit after income tax jumped 22% during the first quarter of the year to 5.4 million ringgit from 4.4 million ringgit a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Revenue, however, declined 5.1% year over year to 46.7 million ringgit from 49.3 million ringgit, due to a decrease in the Edible & Non-Edible Oil Refinery segment.

Oiltek International's order book totaled about 373.4 million ringgit and the orders are expected to be fulfilled over the next 18 to 24 months.

Shares of the integrated process technology and renewable energy solutions provider were up nearly 3% in recent trade.

data.title: China's Broad Money Supply Up 8% as of End-April
data.description:
China's balance of broad money, or M2, rose 8% year over year to 325.2 trillion yuan as of the end of April, according to data from the People's Bank of China released Thursday.

The increase was higher than analysts' expectations for a 7.3% growth, according to a poll by Reuters.

The balance of currency in circulation, or M0, rose 12% to about 13.1 trillion yuan, while the balance of narrow money, or M1, went up 1.5% to 109.1 trillion yuan.

Net cash injection in the first four months of 2025 was 319.3 billion yuan.

data.title: Palm Oil Falls on Weaker Soybean Oil — Market Talk
data.description: Palm oil prices are lower in Asian trade, tracking weakness in soybean oil on the Chicago Board of Trade, says David Ng, a trader at Kuala Lumpur-based Iceberg X. Persistent concerns over rising output and stock levels are also seen weighing on sentiment, he says. CPO production for May 1-10 is estimated to have risen 22.3% on month, according to Malaysia's Southern Peninsular Palm Oil Millers' Association. Ng sees support at 3,750 ringgit a ton and resistance at 3,980 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for July delivery is down by 25 ringgit to 3,898 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: South Korean shares fall as rally from US-China trade talks loses steam
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI falls, foreigners net buyers
  • Korean won strengthens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield rises

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares fell on Thursday as the tech-led rally triggered by U.S.-China trade talks began to lose steam. The won strengthened, while the benchmark bond yield rose.

** The benchmark KOSPI was down 3.57 points, or 0.14%, at 2,637.00 as of 0228 GMT.

** Among index heavyweights, chipmaker Samsung Electronics rose 1.05%, while peer SK Hynix lost 0.24%. Battery maker LG Energy Solution climbed 0.32%.

** Hyundai Motor and sister automaker Kia Corp were down 1.17% and down 0.65%, respectively. Steelmaker POSCO Holdings shed 0.40%, while drugmaker Samsung Biologics fell 0.10%.

** South Korea's deputy finance minister Choi Ji-young met with Assistant Secretary for International Finance at the U.S. Treasury, Robert Kaproth, to discuss the dollar/won market on May 5, a South Korean government official said on Wednesday.

** Of the total 935 traded issues, 320 shares advanced, while 551 declined.

** Foreigners were net buyers of shares worth 96 billion won ($68.6 million).

** The won was quoted at 1,402.9 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.11% higher than its previous close at 1,404.5.

** In offshore trading, the won was quoted at 1,400.3 per dollar, up 0.5% on the day, while in non-deliverable forward trading its one-month contract (KRW1MNDFOR=) was quoted at 1,397.2.

** The KOSPI has risen 9.90% so far this year.

** The won has strengthened 4.9% against the dollar so far this year.

** In money and debt markets, June futures on three-year treasury bonds (KTBc1) lost 0.04 point to 107.51.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) was flat at 2.365%, while the benchmark 10-year yield rose 2.3 basis points to 2.740%.

($1 = 1,400.4500 won)

data.title: Otoki to Invest KRW56.5 Billion in US Subsidiary for Market Expansion
data.description:
Otoki will invest 56.5 billion won in its US subsidiary, Otoki America, in order to expand the local domestic market.

Otoki plans to purchase 400,000 shares of the US subsidiary on June 27, the South Korean food manufacturing company said in a Wednesday regulatory filing with Korea Exchange.

data.title: OptiNose Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• OptiNose Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of $1.92​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $-1.80. The lone analyst forecast for the quarter was for a loss of 74 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 24.4% to $18.51 million from a year ago; analysts expected $17.70 million.

  • OptiNose Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of $1.92​.

  • OptiNose Inc shares had risen by 3.1% this quarter and gained 41.5% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 1.3% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for OptiNose Inc is $9.00

data.title: Battery recycler Li-Cycle files for bankruptcy protection in Canada
data.description:
Canadian lithium battery recycling firm Li-Cycle on Wednesday said that company and its units in North America filed for bankruptcy protection in Canada.

Li-Cycle has entered into a stalking horse credit bid with London listed Glencore for at least $40 million.

data.title: Span Divergent's Limited Liability Partnership Arm Gets Voluntarily Dissolved
data.description:
Diagnostic services provider Span Divergent said its limited liability partnership company, Desai Farmharvest LLP, has been voluntarily dissolved and has ceased to be a subsidiary of the company, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The dissolution took place after receiving approval from the Registrar of Companies to strike off Desai Farmharvest's name from the register of LLPs.

Desai Farmharvest has not been operative since its incorporation and has not undertaken any business operations.

data.title: Surging Australian Jobs Growth in April Could Trouble RBA — Update
data.description:
By James Glynn

SYDNEY--Australian employment growth in April surged by more than four times what economists expected, which could cast doubt on a widely anticipated rate cut by the Reserve Bank of Australia next week.

Employment jumped by 89,000 in April compared with an expected increase of just 21,000, while the unemployment rate was unchanged at 4.1%, the Australian Bureau of Statistics said Thursday.

The big jump in employment follows news on Wednesday of renewed strength in wages growth in the first quarter, and counters the idea that the Australian economy is weak against the backdrop of a slowing global growth outlook.

Employment grew by 390,000, or 2.7%, over the year to April, with the rate of increase higher than population growth, the ABS said.

The jump in employment in April was biased toward full-time work, which grew by 59,500 over the month, the data showed.

The workforce participation rate also jumped to 67.1% in April from 66.8% in March.

The strong employment and wages data will likely spark warnings about Australia's weak productivity growth performance over the last decade, and the potential for inflation to linger in a tight job market.

Economists said the RBA could still cut interest rates next week, citing the fact that both headline and core measures of inflation are back within the 2% to 3% target band.

Still, the strength in employment growth suggests the depth of cuts further out in the year will be shallower.

"With the labor market going from strength to strength, we're more convinced than ever that the RBA will be reluctant to cut rates aggressively," said Abhijit Surya, economist at Capital Economics.

The RBA's easing cycle might be limited to just two more cuts, taking the official cash rate down to 3.60% from 4.10% now, he added.

Ben Jarman, chief economist for Australia at JPMorgan, said he still expects the RBA to announce a cut on Tuesday, but added that the guidance around the decision will "measured."

While the RBA won't rule out further cuts, it won't pre-commit to them either, Jarman added.

Write to James Glynn at james.glynn@wsj.com

data.title: China's Shenghe Resources hits more than 6-month high on buying Aussie miner
data.description:
** Chinese rare-earths producer Shenghe Resources jumps 9.6% to 13.48 yuan, highest since November 7, 2024

** Stock set for biggest one-day pct rise since May 6

** Shenghe Resources will acquire Peak Rare Earths for A$150.5 mln ($96.8 mln), plus the full amount raised under Peak's proposed A$7.5 mln entitlement offer, the Australian firm said

** Shenghe's unit, Ganzhou Chenguang Rare Earths New Material, will buy Peak for a minimum of A$0.359/share in cash, a 199% premium to Peak's last close, sending PEK soaring 150%

** YTD, Shenghe gains ~27%

($1 = A$1.5542)

data.title: China Stocks Decline Amid Lingering Trade Uncertainty
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite fell 0.5% to below 3,390 while the Shenzhen Component dropped 0.8% to 10,270 on Thursday, paring gains from the previous session as persistent trade-related uncertainties weighed on sentiment.

Although Beijing and Washington agreed to a 90-day tariff reduction, analysts cautioned that the absence of a long-term trade agreement continues to cloud China’s economic outlook.

US officials have also emphasized their goal of reducing reliance on Chinese imports, adding to investor caution.

Trade negotiations between the two countries are expected to continue in the coming weeks in an effort to reach a broader deal.

In a positive sign, China lifted its export ban on rare earths targeting US entities, signaling further de-escalation in trade tensions.

Technology stocks led the decline, with notable losses from East Money (-2.3%), Avic Chengdu (-4.9%), Victory Giant (-3.8%), Zhongji Innolight (-1.4%), and Talkweb Information (-3.2%).

data.title: Australian dollar lifted by jobs jump, rate cut still in play
data.description:
The Australian dollar swung higher again on Thursday after a surprisingly strong reading on employment added to the case against rapid-fire rate cuts in coming months, though an easing is still widely expected for next week.

Data showed 89,000 net new jobs were created in April, way above forecasts of 20,000 and continuing a run of wild swings in the series.

The shock result was enough to push bond yields up to four-month highs and lift the Aussie 0.3% to $0.6445 . It had stretched as far as $0.6501 overnight before running into selling.

Support lies around $0.6360, with major resistance at the recent five-month top of $0.6515.

Crucially for wages and inflation, the labour force expanded by even more so that unemployment stayed at 4.1% as expected, showing the supply of labour was meeting strong demand.

As a result, markets were still wagering the Reserve Bank of Australia will cut its 4.10% cash rate by a quarter point at its meeting on May 20, the second easing this year. (0#AUDIRPR)

Beyond that, investors have been scaling back expectations and now see around 76 basis points of easing by year-end compared to more than 100 basis points a couple of weeks ago.

"With the labour market going from strength to strength, we're more convinced than ever that the RBA will be reluctant to cut rates aggressively," said Abhijit Surya, a senior APAC economist at Capital Economics.

"We're sticking to our forecast for a terminal policy rate of 3.60%."

The kiwi dollar steadied at $0.5902 , after recoiling from a high of $0.5969 overnight. Support comes in at $0.5845, with resistance just above $0.6000.

There was some notable volatility on the crosses on reports Washington wanted a weaker U.S. dollar to be part of trade talks with Asian countries, which were then played down.

The local bond market has had a tough week as a selloff in Treasuries spread globally, sending 10-year yields surging 28 basis points to a peak of 4.58%. Three-year bond futures (YTTc1) were down a hefty 29 ticks so far this week to 96.300.

This shift was partly due to the easing of U.S.-China trade tensions which led investors to scale back the risk of a U.S. and global recession, and thus the need for aggressive rate cuts both in the U.S. and at home.

data.title: NCsoft Stock Could Underperform Market — Market Talk
data.description: NCsoft's stock could underperform the market this year, given its limited earnings visibility, weak game pipeline and continuing margin compression, Nomura analysts Angela Hong and Won Kang write in a note. The South Korean videogame developer's underlying fundamentals remain weak on a continued downtrend in mobile and PC game revenue, they say. The analysts slash their 2025 net profit forecast for NCsoft by 26% after lowering their annual revenue projection by 13%. They remain cautious about management's 2026 guidance for double-digit revenue growth. Nomura cuts the stock's target price by 20% to KRW120,000 and keeps a reduce rating. Shares are 1.1% lower at KRW156,800. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0235 GMT - NCsoft's stock could underperform the market this year, given its limited earnings visibility, weak game pipeline and continuing margin compression, Nomura analysts Angela Hong and Won Kang write in a note. The South Korean videogame developer's underlying fundamentals remain weak on a continued downtrend in mobile and PC game revenue, they say. The analysts slash their 2025 net profit forecast for NCsoft by 26% after lowering their annual revenue projection by 13%. They remain cautious about management's 2026 guidance for double-digit revenue growth. Nomura cuts the stock's target price by 20% to KRW120,000 and keeps a reduce rating. Shares are 1.1% lower at KRW156,800. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0219 GMT - Thai Beverage's outlook appears challenging after its 1H FY 2025 results missed expectations, UOB Kay Hian analysts say in a research report. For 2H FY 2025, the brokerage has become more pessimistic on the beverage company's beer segment owing to recent macroeconomic uncertainty that has hurt sales volumes, specifically that of Vietnamese subsidiary Sabeco. The Singapore-listed company's overall sales volume is likely to only grow by a low single-digit percentage in 2H FY 2025, partly due to a weakening Thai economy, the analysts add. The brokerage downgrades the stock's rating to hold from buy and lowers the target price to S$0.51 from S$0.56. Shares are 1.0% lower at S$0.475. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0209 GMT - Tencent's AI investment should position it well for future growth, Barclays analysts say in a research note. While AI has tangibly benefited Tencent's ads and games sectors, it's a discovery process for both the company and users in other areas, the analysts say. In response to the recent Nvidia H20 export restriction, management noted during the earnings call that it's capable of pursing its AI strategies using previously acquired chips. The analysts think that "management recognized the fluid situation with regards to chip procurement [and] therefore has been allocating these chips to areas where immediate tangible returns can be achieved." Management believes that as its computing requirements gradually move to inferencing, they can use non-Nvidia chips to fulfill the growing needs. Shares are up 0.2% at HK$522.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0201 GMT - Genting Singapore's operations should be stronger from 3Q, Maybank IB analyst Yin Shao Yang writes in a note. The Singapore Oceanarium as well as The Laurus, an all-suite resort, will be opened by then, Yin says. "The latter is especially important as it will add to the room inventory to house VIP and premium mass players," Yin says. The integrated resorts operator's 1Q earnings were in line with Maybank's expectations. VIP volume and mass market recovered meaningfully on quarter, Yin adds. Maybank maintains a buy rating on the stock and the target price of S$1.01. Shares are down 2.0% at S$0.72.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0153 GMT - Hong Leong Bank is expected to weather global trade tensions, given its minimal export exposure and strong retail-driven, secured loan portfolio, Kenanga IB analyst Clement Chua says in a note. He reckons that the lender's MYR500 million overlay could provide a strong credit risk buffer. The outlook for its associate, Bank of Chengdu, also appears stable, supported by a focus on state-backed loans, he adds. Kenanga lowers Hong Leong Bank's target price to MYR24.50 from MYR27.40 after revising its valuation method to separately assess Bank of Chengdu and Hong Leong Bank's domestic operations, but maintains an outperform rating, on the bank's stability and above-industry returns. Shares are unchanged at MYR20.20. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0152 GMT - Prime US REIT may benefit from expected major lease signings, RHB Research's Vijay Natarajan says in a research report. The REIT has gotten letters of intent from two large tenants, which it expects to convert into lease agreements by 3Q, the analyst notes. The leases, if signed, would contribute positively from FY 2026 and will probably lead to substantial asset valuation uplift, given cash-flow visibility, the analyst says. Also, the REIT is in negotiations with five prospective tenants as strong quality of its assets in the U.S.' sub-markets continues to attract tenants. However, RHB trims its target price to US$0.18 from US$0.23, given elevated macroeconomic uncertainties, with unchanged buy rating. Units are 2.8% lower at US$0.14. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0150 GMT - Sunway REIT's growth outlook is likely to remain upbeat, supported by recent acquisitions, ongoing initiatives to improve assets and coming developments like Sunway Pier, Maybank Investment Bank analyst Nur Farah Syifaa says in a note. She expects the retail segment to stay resilient, with high occupancy and scalable rental growth, while the hospitality segment should rebound in 2Q on an expected pick up in demand for meetings, conventions, exhibitions and leisure stays. Gearing is expected to ease to around 40%, compared with 1Q's 42%, she adds. Maybank maintains a buy rating on Sunway REIT and keeps its target price at MYR2.13. Shares are 2.6% higher at MYR1.98. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0127 GMT - Venture Corp. is likely to face sluggish growth this year, Phillip Securities Research's Paul Chew says in a commentary. The Singapore-listed technology solutions provider's 1Q earnings missed expectations, and its guidance remains cautious for the rest of 2025, the head of research says. The company mentioned that there is significant uncertainty in the global economic environment owing to tariffs, and there is no clear visibility in the tariffs' landscape over the next year, Chew adds. The brokerage cuts its 2025 revenue estimate for Venture by 5%. It also lowers the stock's target price to S$10.40 from S$11.80, with an unchanged neutral rating. Shares are 0.2% lower at S$11.25. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0109 GMT - HMM is set to get an earnings boost from higher U.S.- and Europe-bound freight rates this year, Daishin Securities analyst Yang Ji-hwan writes in a note. Rates have increased roughly 10%-15% from 2024. The South Korean shipping company also stands to gain from a possible increase in cargo traffic following the recent U.S.-China agreement on tariff reductions, Yang says. The analyst raises his 2025 operating-profit forecast for HMM by 21%. He is also upbeat on HMM's planned KRW2 trillion share buyback. Daishin raises the stock's target price by 4.5% to KRW23,000 and keeps a market-perform rating. Shares are 6.0% higher at KRW22,050. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0108 GMT - Genting Singapore's 1Q results appear to be a mixed bag, Citi Research analysts write in a note. The integrated resort operator's gross gaming revenue was better-than-expected, but non-gaming revenues were underwhelming despite the opening of "Minion Land" in February, they say. "We believe that is reflecting Singapore's overall visitation and the market [revenue per available room] trends," they add. The analysts maintain a buy rating on the stock and target price of S$1.14, as they reckon that the new phases for Resorts World Sentosa will support Genting Singapore's earnings growth. Shares are 1.4% lower at S$0.73.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0103 GMT - Indonesia's recent hike in crude palm oil export levies may support global CPO prices and strengthen the country's biodiesel mandate, RHB analysts say in a note. They think the policy could improve financial stability for Indonesia's B40 biodiesel program, if oil prices hold above $49/bbl, compared with current $82/bbl, as well as potential for a B50 mandate. Although Indonesian planters may see lower effective average selling prices, higher CPO prices could offset the impact, they reckon. The edge that downstream refiners in Indonesia have would narrow and may enhance Malaysian companies' downstream competitiveness, they say. RHB maintains an overweight rating on Southeast Asia's plantation sector, pegs London Sumatra Indonesia, Bumitama Agri, SD Guthrie, Johor Plantations, and Sarawak Oil Palms as top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0020 GMT - Xero's annual earnings have something for both bulls and bears to get their teeth into, RBC Capital Markets analyst Garry Sherriff says. Sherriff, who has an outperform rating on the stock, points to stronger-than-expected free cashflow and earnings as key positives in the cloud-accounting software provider's fiscal 2025 result. For the bears, he tells clients in a note that Xero's expense-ratio guidance is higher than the average analyst forecast. RBC has a A$185.00 target price on the stock, which is down 1.8% at A$170.71. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Japan futures snap 10-day winning streak on trade uncertainty, supply outlook
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures snapped a 10-day winning streak on Thursday, as market uncertainty surrounding Sino-U.S. trade relations weighed on sentiment, while improving weather conditions in top producer Thailand raised supply prospects.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for October delivery , nudged down 2.5 yen, or 0.78%, at 317.1 yen ($2.17) per kg, as of 0217 GMT.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for September delivery eased 35 yuan, or 0.23%, to 15,120 yuan ($2,094.91) per metric ton.

  • The most active June butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) fell 35 yuan, or 0.28%, to 12,265 yuan ($1,699.34) per metric ton.

  • Thawing in trade tensions between China and the United States has brought some support to the rubber market, said Chinese financial information site Tonghuashun Information.

  • On Monday, the U.S. and China agreed to pause their trade war for at least 90 days.

  • Still, a pullback in rubber prices on Thursday reflects market uncertainty, as investors are unsure how policies will evolve after the 90-day truce, added Tonghuashun.

  • The monsoon prevailing over the South will weaken with less rains from May 18-20, said Thailand's meteorological agency.

  • Oil prices lost nearly $1 in early trade on expectations of a potential U.S.-Iran nuclear deal.

  • Natural rubber often takes direction from oil prices as it competes for market share with synthetic rubber, which is made from crude oil.

  • Meanwhile, the Japanese yen strengthened a bit to 146.48 per dollar but remained close to the one-month low of 148.65 touched earlier this week.

  • A weaker currency makes yen-denominated assets more affordable to overseas buyers.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for June delivery last traded at 175.8 U.S. cents per kg, down 0.8%.

($1 = 146.2800 yen)

($1 = 7.2175 Chinese yuan)
